hero.title: Clinical clarity for presentations that resist simple answers.
hero.lead: An in-depth medical approach to understanding symptoms that appear across several systems at once, integrating evidence and building individualized direction.
hero.credentials: Carine Petry · CRM-DF 15342 · RQE 16243 / 12865 · Sleep Medicine specialist

manifesto.title: Your symptoms deserve to be understood together.
body: If you have been told that "your tests are normal" while your body insisted something was wrong, this is for you. Complex presentations are rarely explained by a single system. Symptoms of the skin, digestion, circulation, sleep and cognition may share one underlying mechanism and, split across separate specialties, that mechanism stays invisible.

Reading the clinical history as a whole is what makes the pattern recognizable. It is work of listening, correlation and method, carried out with the time complexity requires. Investigating in an integrated way is not ordering more tests: it is interpreting the whole.

profile.title: A practice dedicated to multisystem and underdiagnosed presentations.
body: Carine Petry is a physician and Sleep Medicine specialist whose practice is dedicated to the clinical investigation of rare and frequently underdiagnosed conditions: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome (MCAS), Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (EDS) and the persistent effects of COVID-19.

The practice is organized around three commitments: a clinical history detailed enough to reconstruct the trajectory of the symptom, correlation across systems before any conclusion, and transparency about the limits of what can be asserted. No promise medicine cannot keep.
profile.philosophy: To investigate is to hold uncertainty for as long as it takes for evidence to emerge, and to recognize, when it applies, that the right direction lies with another specialty.

method.title: How the investigation is conducted.
method.lead: A defined path, with stages that build on one another. Each consultation begins where the previous one ended.

steps.d: Reconstructing the trajectory: when each symptom appeared, what preceded it, how it evolved and what has already been investigated. The order of events often points to the mechanism.
Joint analysis of skin, digestive tract, cardiovascular system, sleep and cognition. Symptoms treated as separate fragments rarely reveal what they share.
Tests chosen from defined hypotheses, with attention to the correct moment of collection. In some conditions, the result depends on when the sample is taken.
Reasoned direction, with follow-up of the response and, when the presentation calls for another specialty, the appropriate referral.

areasHome.title: Conditions investigated.
areasHome.lead: Four areas of investigation that frequently overlap in the same patient and are therefore assessed together.

items.d: Reactions involving skin, digestive tract, cardiovascular and nervous systems at once, without allergy testing explaining the picture.
items.signals: Flushing · reactions to foods and medications · symptoms triggered by heat, exertion or stress

items.d: Connective tissue alterations with joint, cutaneous and systemic repercussions, assessed against current international criteria.
items.signals: Joint hypermobility · chronic pain · recurrent dislocations · fatigue

items.d: Symptoms persisting months after infection, with multisystem mechanisms under active investigation in the scientific literature.
items.signals: Persistent fatigue · post-exertional malaise · cognitive changes · non-restorative sleep

items.d: Assessment of sleep architecture and quality as a structural axis, frequently the element that reorganizes the understanding of a complex case.
items.signals: Non-restorative sleep · apnea · chronic insomnia · excessive daytime sleepiness

authority.title: Rigor demonstrated, not declared.
authority.lead: Verifiable training, output and participation. Nothing in this section is published without documentary evidence.
authority.pending: CONTENT_REQUIRED: publications, conference participation, teaching and professional affiliations awaiting supporting documentation. See docs/CONTENT_REQUIRED.md.

items.label: Registered specialty
items.d: Sleep Medicine, with practice in the clinical investigation of multisystem presentations.

items.label: Basis of conduct
items.d: Current international diagnostic criteria for the conditions investigated, with continuous review of the literature.

journey.title: What to expect from the first contact.

items.d: People with persistent, multisystem symptoms who have already been assessed without an integrated explanation of the picture.
Gather previous test results in chronological order, including those that came back unremarkable, and record the complete list of symptoms.
A list of medications and supplements in use with doses, a history of reactions and a timeline of symptoms.
The request is received by the team, who provide guidance on the investigation and scheduling options.

clinic.title: Where the consultation takes place.
clinic.lead: Centro Médico Lucio Costa, Asa Sul, Brasília. Accessibility and parking information on the contact page.

articles.title: Science translated with precision.
articles.lead: Texts that explain mechanisms and criteria without simplifications that distort.
articles.recentTitle: What the evidence shows, updated regularly
articles.recentLead: Clear summaries on multisystem conditions, written for those who live with them and for those who investigate them.
articles.disclaimer: Informational content. It does not replace individualized medical assessment and does not establish a diagnosis.

items.q: When does an assessment like this make sense?
items.a: When persistent symptoms involve more than one body system without an explanation that integrates the whole, particularly after previous assessments that did not reach a clear direction.

items.q: How should I organize my test results?
items.a: In chronological order, with visible dates, including complete results rather than only the final report. Unremarkable results are relevant clinical information too.

items.q: Are patients from other regions seen?
items.a: Yes. The practice is in Brasília and receives patients from other locations. The team advises on scheduling at first contact.

items.q: What if my case belongs to another specialty?
items.a: Referral is part of clinical conduct. Recognizing that a presentation belongs to another field is part of medical responsibility, not a failure of the investigation.

items.q: What happens after I submit a request?
items.a: The team gets in touch to understand the case and provide guidance on the investigation and scheduling options.

finalCta.title: If you recognize your history here, the first step is a conversation.
finalCta.lead: Send your information and the team will get in touch with guidance on the investigation and scheduling.
finalCta.note: The team responds on business days.

lp.symptomsNote: This list is informational and is not a self-diagnosis instrument. Isolated signs do not characterize the condition. Medical assessment considers the whole picture, its evolution and the applicable diagnostic criteria.
lp.formLead: Briefly describe your case. The team will get in touch with guidance on the investigation and scheduling options.
lp.medicalDisclaimer: This form does not perform clinical triage, does not establish a diagnosis and does not replace a medical consultation. Information is used solely for contact and scheduling guidance, under Brazilian data protection law (LGPD).

form.title: Request information
form.subtitle: Marked fields are required.

form.duration: How long you have had these symptoms
